title: VETERANS AND FAMILY CENTER - 211info
text: SALVATION ARMY VETERANS AND FAMILY CENTER VETERANS AND FAMILY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a transitional housing center offering a supportive environment for up to 60 veterans and their families for stays of up to 9 months. Services include case management, skill building, job training assistance, sober living quarters, mentoring, meals, and benefits assistance. Individuals must attend a 1.5-hour orientation, pass a UA and breathalyzer test upon entry, be able to care for themselves independently, and agree to remain drug- and alcohol-free during their stay. 14825 SW Farmington Road Beaverton, OR 97007 Eligibility: Veterans who are experiencing or at the risk of being homeless Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 731-3951 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 239-1259 Main phone Front Desk to Veteran and Family Center (503) 731-3951 Main phone Main phone (503) 731-3951 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information. Must be referred by the VA. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: ID if available AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Main cross streets are Murray Boulevard and Farmington Road in Beaverton Services: Transitional Housing/Shelter * Homeless Veterans If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. SALVATION ARMY VETERANS AND FAMILY CENTER
